en.commentary:
The SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless earns the top rank because it solves the practical problem of wireless gaming more completely than any other headset at any price. The hot-swap dual battery system is the standout engineering decision — one battery runs in the headset while the other charges in the OLED base station, and swapping takes under ten seconds. Combine that with parametric EQ, ANC that meaningfully reduces ambient noise, and the ClearCast Gen 2 microphone's AI noise suppression, and you have a headset where every feature serves a real purpose. At $349 it demands a commitment, and it delivers on it.

The Audeze Maxwell 2 ranks second, and in terms of raw audio fidelity it is genuinely first. The 90mm planar magnetic drivers produce soundstage width and instrument separation that no dynamic driver headset can replicate. Audeze added Bluetooth 5.3 LDAC at CES 2026, which means high-resolution audio over Bluetooth without quality loss for the first time in a gaming headset at this price. I rank it second rather than first because the weight and the redesigned headband drew legitimate comfort complaints in extended testing across multiple publications. A gaming headset you stop wearing after two hours has failed at its core function. If audio quality is the only variable, the Maxwell 2 is first.

The Logitech G Pro X 2 Lightspeed is the competitive gaming standard. Graphene drivers deliver precise directional audio where footstep location matters. Fifty hours of battery survives a full week of daily sessions without a charge. Lightspeed wireless consistently tests under 20ms. It makes no attempt to compete on ANC or audiophile audio, and that focus is exactly why it succeeds at what competitive gamers actually need.

The HyperX Cloud Alpha Wireless at rank 5 is the most underrated headset on this list. Three hundred hours of wireless battery life is not a marketing claim but an engineering feat — the result of deliberately limiting feature scope to maximize cell capacity. For casual gamers who play irregularly and want a headset that simply has charge whenever they reach for it, nothing else comes close.

The Corsair HS80 RGB Wireless at $100 and the Razer BlackShark V2 at $70 cover the value tier with conviction. The HS80's sub-1ms Slipstream wireless at that price is genuinely unusual — most headsets in this range use Bluetooth-only with higher and less consistent latency. The BlackShark V2 trades wireless entirely for audio maximization, and the TriForce titanium drivers make that tradeoff worthwhile.

highlights.title: SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless Is the Most Complete Gaming Headset Built
highlights.body: The dual battery system is the feature that makes everything else possible. One battery charges in the OLED base station while the other powers the headset, and the swap takes under ten seconds with no audio interruption. This is fundamentally different from a headset with a long-lasting single battery — it eliminates the charge management problem entirely rather than extending it. The parametric EQ is full-featured with a visual curve editor. The ANC is effective enough to cut meaningful ambient noise. The ClearCast Gen 2 mic AI suppression removes background noise from the voice signal. At $349, this is the headset I recommend without qualification to anyone who games seriously on PC or PlayStation.

highlights.title: Audeze Maxwell 2 Is for Gamers Who Treat Audio as the Primary Investment
highlights.body: The 90mm planar magnetic driver is the largest in any gaming headset and produces a soundstage that closed-back dynamic driver designs cannot replicate. Instrument separation in games with layered audio design — orchestral soundtracks, complex ambience, competitive audio mixes — is noticeably wider than on any other headset on this list. The LDAC addition at CES 2026 means this is now also a legitimate hi-res Bluetooth headset for music. The honest caveat is weight and the redesigned headband, which drew negative comfort feedback from multiple extended-use reviewers. This is the correct recommendation for short-session audiophile gamers, not for six-hour marathon sessions.

highlights.title: HyperX Cloud Alpha Wireless 300-Hour Battery Is a Category-Defining Engineering Decision
highlights.body: No competitor has come within 150 hours of this figure. The 300-hour specification is the result of engineering discipline — the Cloud Alpha Wireless deliberately omits ANC and multi-system wireless to achieve this battery capacity using the same 2.4GHz RF chipset as competitors. For the casual gaming audience that picks up a headset once or twice a week and never thinks about charging schedules, this headset solves a real friction point that higher-spec alternatives do not address. The dual-chamber 50mm drivers deliver audio quality well above what the price might suggest.

highlights.title: ASUS ROG Delta II Offers 110-Hour Battery With Full Tri-Mode Wireless
highlights.body: The ROG Delta II's 110-hour battery is the second-longest on this list and arrives with a feature set that most competitors cut to achieve longevity: tri-mode connectivity covers 2.4GHz ROG SpeedNova, Bluetooth, and 3.5mm wired simultaneously. The titanium-plated 50mm drivers and 10mm super-wideband microphone both sit above the baseline for this price tier. At $230, the Delta II occupies a clear space in the market — more endurance than any competing headset at this price, more features than headsets chasing battery life alone.

highlights.title: Corsair HS80 and Razer BlackShark V2 Anchor the Value Tier With Different Tradeoffs
highlights.body: The Corsair HS80 RGB Wireless is the strongest value in the $100 tier because Slipstream 2.4GHz wireless at this price is rare — most competing headsets use Bluetooth-only connections with higher and less predictable latency. Dolby Atmos and a 10-band parametric EQ round out a feature set that typically appears in $150-plus headsets. The Razer BlackShark V2 makes the opposite bet: no wireless at all, with the full $70 budget redirected to TriForce titanium drivers and THX Spatial Audio. For wired desktop setups where cable management is not a concern, the BlackShark V2 delivers audio quality that its price does not predict.

zh-tw.commentary:
電競耳機在 2026 年的市場格局很清楚，SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless 放第一是我最沒有猶豫的結論。雙電池熱插拔系統是這個品類裡唯一真正解決充電問題的設計，一顆電池在耳機裡跑，一顆在 OLED 底座充，換電不需要暫停，十秒以內換好繼續玩。把 ANC 主動降噪、參數化 EQ、ClearCast Gen 2 麥克風 AI 降噪這三個功能同時塞進來，NT$11,990 在電競耳機的功能密度確實是頂端。

Audeze Maxwell 2 放第二，但音質排名我會給它第一。90mm 平面磁力驅動器的音場寬度和樂器分離度是動圈驅動器做不到的事，CES 2026 加入 LDAC 讓無線也能傳輸高解析音訊。把它排第二的理由很直接，幾家媒體測試都提到新版頭帶舒適度不如前代，戴兩個小時就需要休息的耳機不管音質多好都不能排第一。短時間精聽或只在乎音質的使用場景，Maxwell 2 毫無疑問是最頂的選擇。

Logitech G Pro X 2 Lightspeed 是給競技玩家看的選項。石墨烯驅動器讓腳步聲和方位定位比多數同價位耳機精準，50 小時電池一個禮拜天天玩都不需要充電，Lightspeed 無線實測低於 20ms 延遲。這款的設計重心非常集中在競技場景，沒有試圖在 ANC 或音樂鑑賞力上和頂端競品比，這個取捨是正確的。

HyperX Cloud Alpha Wireless 的 300 小時電池是真正的技術特例。沒有其他耳機接近這個數字，這是刻意犧牲其他功能換來的工程選擇。對於打電動不規律、習慣把耳機擺著忘記充電的玩家，這款耳機幾乎是量身定做的解答。

Corsair HS80 RGB Wireless 在 NT$3,290 用 Slipstream 2.4GHz 做到低於 1ms 延遲，這在這個價位很難得，多數同價位無線耳機用藍牙，延遲較高也不穩定。Razer BlackShark V2 放棄無線換音質，NT$2,290 買到 TriForce 鈦金屬驅動器，這個取捨對於不需要無線的桌面玩家是值得的。

highlights.title: SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless 放第一，雙電池設計是真正的理由
highlights.body: 不是某個規格特別突出，而是所有功能同時都做到很好的水準，這在電競耳機裡很難。雙電池熱插拔讓充電這件事從此不是問題，OLED 底座讓你同時管理兩台設備之間的切換，ANC 降噪在測試中可以壓掉大多數鍵盤聲和環境噪音，ClearCast Gen 2 麥克風 AI 降噪讓語音在吵雜環境裡也很清楚。NT$11,990 在電競耳機不便宜，但你在一個產品裡拿到的功能密度對得起這個價格，我推薦這款是認真的。

highlights.title: Audeze Maxwell 2 是給認真聽音的玩家，不是給追求最佳整體體驗的玩家
highlights.body: 90mm 平面磁力驅動器的音質確實是另一個等級，聽音樂、看電影、或是玩音場設計細緻的遊戲，Maxwell 2 的聲音讓你感覺耳機的錢花得值。問題是平面磁力設計比較重，加上新版頭帶的評價不如前代，長時間配戴的舒適度在好幾家媒體的評測裡都被點名。這款適合短時間精聽、音場型遊戲、或是把電競耳機當高音質耳機用的使用場景，六小時馬拉松式遊戲不是它的強項。如果你最在乎音質，Maxwell 2 選它。

highlights.title: HyperX Cloud Alpha Wireless 的 300 小時電池是這個品類的真正特例
highlights.body: 這不是行銷說法，是刻意放棄 ANC 和多設備切換功能換來的工程結果，沒有其他耳機的電池接近這個數字。對於不規律玩家，常常把耳機擺著忘記充電，然後想玩的時候才想到要確認有沒有電，Cloud Alpha Wireless 的使用場景是為你設計的，兩個禮拜不充電拿起來還有電。雙腔體驅動器的音質在 NT$4,990 這個價位也比外觀看起來的規格表要好，是一款被低估的選項。

highlights.title: ASUS ROG Delta II 在台灣的優勢是品牌在地化服務加上超長電池
highlights.body: 台灣買 ASUS 有個實際好處，出問題不需要跨境寄修，在地維修和客服流程完整。ROG Delta II 本身的 110 小時電池在這個價位段是最長的之一，NT$7,490 買到鈦金屬驅動器加上三模無線連接，涵蓋 2.4GHz、藍牙、3.5mm 有線，切換彈性在這個名單裡算高的。如果你在乎售後保固有實體支援，或是同時在 PC 和 PS5 之間切換，Delta II 是最務實的選擇。

highlights.title: Corsair HS80 的低延遲無線和 Razer BlackShark V2 的音質，是兩種不同的預算正確解答
highlights.body: Corsair HS80 RGB Wireless 在 NT$3,290 用 Slipstream 2.4GHz 做到低於 1ms 延遲，這在這個價位很難得，因為同價位多數無線耳機用藍牙，延遲較高也不穩定。Dolby Atmos 讓音場立體感在遊戲裡有實際體感差距。Razer BlackShark V2 的方向完全相反，NT$2,290 放棄無線連接把所有資源放在 TriForce 鈦金屬驅動器和 THX 空間音效，對於桌面固定使用、不需要無線的玩家，這個取捨在音質上的回報是值得的。

en.commentary:
SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless holds rank 1 in April 2026, and this position is driven by a combination that no single competitor has matched: simultaneous dual wireless connectivity, an active noise cancellation module that's effective enough to use in real environments, and the hot-swap battery system that eliminates charging interruptions entirely. These features together define what a flagship gaming headset should deliver in 2026, and the Nova Pro Wireless delivers all three without compromise.

Audeze Maxwell 2 earns rank 2 as the best headset for audio quality specifically. The planar magnetic drivers produce a soundstage and detail retrieval that dynamic driver headsets in this price range simply don't match. For buyers who also listen to music seriously outside gaming, the Maxwell 2 justifies its cost as a dual-purpose device.

The Turtle Beach Stealth Pro II enters this ranking at rank 3, announced in April 2026 and launching May 17. The 60mm Eclipse dual drivers with Japan Audio Society Hi-Res Audio certification and 24-bit/96kHz wireless are the key differentiators at this price. The dual 40-hour swappable battery system and CrossPlay 2.0 simultaneous multiplatform switching make this a direct challenger to the Nova Pro Wireless at the same $349.99. Pre-release testing and spec analysis put it firmly in the premium tier.

Logitech G Pro X 2 Lightspeed holds rank 4 on competitive gaming credentials. The graphene drivers and sub-20ms Lightspeed wireless remain the benchmark for esports-focused audio. The gaming headset market's premium tier has never been more competitive — the Stealth Pro II's entry means buyers now have three credible flagship options at or near the $350 mark.

highlights.title: Nova Pro Wireless's Hot-Swap Battery System Is Still the Engineering Benchmark
highlights.body: The hot-swap dual battery in a base station dock remains the most complete charging solution in gaming headsets. You swap a fresh cell in under 10 seconds without removing the headset. The Turtle Beach Stealth Pro II's dual 40-hour swappable batteries are competitive, but the Nova Pro's OLED base station with simultaneous device management tips the balance for the most demanding users.

highlights.title: Turtle Beach Stealth Pro II Enters the Premium Tier With Spec-Matching Confidence
highlights.body: The 60mm Eclipse dual drivers certified for Hi-Res Audio at 24-bit/96kHz are larger than the industry standard 50mm drivers in most competitors. CrossPlay 2.0 handles simultaneous 2.4GHz and Bluetooth with one-click multiplatform switching for Xbox, PlayStation, PC, and mobile. At $349.99 against the Nova Pro Wireless at the same price, the Stealth Pro II gives the premium tier genuine competition for the first time.

highlights.title: Audeze Maxwell 2 Planar Magnetic Drivers Are in a Different Sonic Class
highlights.body: Planar magnetic technology produces a flatter frequency response, wider stereo imaging, and more accurate transient response than dynamic drivers. For games where spatial audio and footstep accuracy matter, the Maxwell 2's soundstage delivers a genuine competitive advantage. The LDAC addition now makes it a legitimate hi-res Bluetooth headset for serious music listening as well.

highlights.title: Logitech G Pro X 2 Lightspeed Wins on All-Day Comfort and Competitive Precision
highlights.body: At 88g wireless, the G Pro X 2 is the lightest high-performance wireless headset in this list. Long-session comfort is determined primarily by weight and clamping force, and the G Pro X 2's combination puts it ahead of heavier competitors for buyers who wear headsets eight or more hours daily. The graphene driver precision for positional audio in competitive FPS titles remains unmatched at this weight class.

zh-tw.commentary:
SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless 在 2026 年四月繼續坐穩電競耳機第一名。雙無線同時連接、真正有效的主動降噪、電池熱插拔系統，這三個功能組合在一起定義了旗艦電競耳機應該有的水準，Nova Pro Wireless 三樣都做到了。

Audeze Maxwell 2 第二名，音質面向的最頂選擇。平面磁性驅動單元在音場寬度和細節解析上是動圈設計做不到的，LDAC 高解析無線讓它在認真聽音樂的場景也站得住腳。

Turtle Beach Stealth Pro II 全新進榜排在第三，預計 5 月 17 日正式開賣。60mm Eclipse 雙驅動器拿到日本音響協會 Hi-Res Audio 認證，24-bit/96kHz 無線傳輸、CrossPlay 2.0 多平台同步切換、雙 40 小時可換電池，在同樣 NT$11,000 的價位直接挑戰 Nova Pro Wireless 的地位，規格紙面上是真正的旗艦競品，等 5 月正式評測出來後排名可能還會再調整。

Logitech G Pro X 2 Lightspeed 第四名，競技場景的標竿選擇。88g 無線重量、Lightspeed 低延遲、石墨烯驅動器的方位定位精準度，對於每天長時間配戴打電動的競技玩家來說，舒適度和腳步聲表現的組合仍然是首選起點。

highlights.title: Nova Pro Wireless 的底座熱插拔依然是充電解決方案的工程基準
highlights.body: OLED 底座讓你在不摘耳機的情況下 10 秒換好充飽的電池，同時管理兩台設備切換。Turtle Beach Stealth Pro II 的雙 40 小時可換電池很有競爭力，但 Nova Pro 的底座生態系對最高要求的用戶來說仍然略勝一籌。

highlights.title: Turtle Beach Stealth Pro II 以旗艦規格正式挑戰頂端市場
highlights.body: 60mm Eclipse 雙驅動器比多數競品的 50mm 標準尺寸更大，拿到 Hi-Res Audio 認證的 24-bit/96kHz 無線傳輸在電競耳機裡是少見的規格。CrossPlay 2.0 讓 Xbox、PS5、PC、手機可以同時連接並一鍵切換。NT$11,000 對上同價位的 Nova Pro Wireless，這是近年頂端電競耳機市場難得出現的真正競爭。

highlights.title: Audeze Maxwell 2 平面磁性的音場在這份榜單裡是另一個等級
highlights.body: 平面磁性技術帶來更平坦的頻率響應、更寬的立體聲音場和更準確的瞬態響應。需要空間音效定位的競技射擊玩家，Maxwell 2 的聲音帶來的是真實的競技優勢，加上 LDAC 讓它認真聽音樂也完全說得過去。

highlights.title: Logitech G Pro X 2 Lightspeed 在長時間佩戴舒適度和競技精準度上同時勝出
highlights.body: 88g 無線版本是這份榜單最輕的高效能無線耳機，石墨烯驅動器的腳步聲定位精準度對競技玩家有實際的勝負影響。每天戴著打八小時以上的電競選手，這款的重量和夾頭力組合提供了最長時間配戴舒適的選項。

references.title: Best Gaming Headsets 2026: Tested for Every Platform
SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless Review 2026
Best Gaming Headsets of 2026: Our Top Picks

en.commentary: SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless continues to lead in April 2026, driven by the most complete feature set in wireless gaming headsets: hi-res audio certification, active noise cancellation that functions in quiet environments, simultaneous multi-platform wireless connectivity, and a hot-swap battery system that eliminates downtime. The sum of these features is what separates it from the Audeze Maxwell 2, which earns second on audio quality grounds alone. The Maxwell 2's planar magnetic drivers deliver a level of audio accuracy that dynamic driver headsets cannot match, but the battery life and weight are compromises relative to the SteelSeries. Turtle Beach Stealth Pro II rounds out the top three with the best microphone quality in the category.

highlights.title: Nova Pro Wireless Hot-Swap Battery Eliminates Charging Interruptions
highlights.body: The dual-battery hot-swap system means you never have to stop gaming to charge. This solves the most frustrating real-world limitation of wireless headsets and alone justifies the price premium over single-battery alternatives.

highlights.title: Audeze Maxwell 2's Planar Magnetics Are Audibly Superior
highlights.body: Planar magnetic drivers reproduce transients and spatial audio with a level of accuracy that dynamic drivers at any price cannot match. For competitive players who use positional audio to track opponents, the Maxwell 2's imaging is a genuine performance advantage.

highlights.title: Turtle Beach Stealth Pro II Has the Best Microphone
highlights.body: Superhuman Hearing processing plus a TruSpeak microphone with beamforming noise rejection produces the clearest voice communication quality in this category. For players who value team communication as much as audio quality, the Stealth Pro II is the correct choice.

zh-tw.commentary: SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless 在 2026 年 4 月繼續排第一，功能完整度是這個分類最高的：Hi-Res 音訊認證、日常環境下有效的主動降噪、同時雙平台無線連線，加上熱插拔電池完全解決無線耳機的充電中斷問題。Audeze Maxwell 2 以純音質排第二，平面振膜驅動單元的聲音精準度是動圈單元追不上的，競技玩家用方位音效追蹤敵人位置的話，Maxwell 2 的聲場是真正的競技優勢，但電池壽命和重量是代價。Turtle Beach Stealth Pro II 的麥克風品質是這個分類最好的。

highlights.title: Nova Pro Wireless 熱插拔電池是無線耳機的革命
highlights.body: 雙電池熱插拔系統讓你永遠不需要停下來充電，這是無線耳機最令人抓狂的限制，Nova Pro Wireless 直接解決了它。

highlights.title: Audeze Maxwell 2 的平面振膜聲音精準度無可取代
highlights.body: 平面振膜的瞬態反應和空間音效精準度是動圈單元在任何價位都達不到的。競技遊戲靠方位音效追蹤敵人的玩家，Maxwell 2 的聲場是真正的勝負差距。

highlights.title: Turtle Beach Stealth Pro II 麥克風品質最佳
highlights.body: Superhuman Hearing 處理加上 TruSpeak 波束成型麥克風，語音通訊清晰度是這個分類最高的。如果你把隊友溝通看得和音質一樣重要，Stealth Pro II 是正確選擇。

references.title: Best gaming headsets 2026: wireless and wired tested

en.commentary: SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless keeps the #1 position in gaming headsets. The feature set score of 9.5 reflects the dual-wireless system — simultaneous 2.4GHz and Bluetooth connections mean you can be connected to your PC and phone at the same time, with seamless audio priority switching. The Active Noise Cancellation mode, while not at consumer headphone level, is functional for blocking out room noise during sessions. Hot-swappable battery system means downtime is measured in seconds. Audeze Maxwell 2 at #2 has the highest audio quality score in this list at 9.8 — the planar magnetic drivers reproduce game audio and music with a detail retrieval that dynamic drivers simply cannot match. If pure audio fidelity is the deciding criterion, Maxwell 2 is the correct choice regardless of ranking position. Turtle Beach Stealth Pro II at #3 matches the Arctis Nova Pro feature set at a comparable price — the choice between these two is mostly about ear cup geometry and how each fits your head. Razer BlackShark V2 at #9 is the wired budget option: 9.5 comfort score at a budget price makes it the best value in this list for wired-preferred users.

highlights.title: SteelSeries dual-wireless lets you stay connected to two devices simultaneously
highlights.body: The Nova Pro Wireless maintains simultaneous 2.4GHz and Bluetooth connections with audio priority logic that routes game audio to the 2.4GHz channel and handles incoming calls through Bluetooth. Switching between audio sources happens in under 1 second. For streamers or users who take frequent calls while gaming, this connectivity architecture is the reason to choose Nova Pro over competitors.

highlights.title: Audeze Maxwell 2 planar magnetic drivers deliver audio quality gaming headsets don't usually reach
highlights.body: Planar magnetic drivers use a thin diaphragm tensioned across a magnetic field — a design that produces lower harmonic distortion and more linear frequency response than dynamic drivers. In listening tests, the Maxwell 2 resolves spatial audio cues, instrument separation, and voice clarity at a level that makes the best dynamic-driver headsets sound slightly compressed by comparison.

highlights.title: Razer BlackShark V2 wired comfort is exceptional for the price
highlights.body: At its price, the BlackShark V2 uses a memory foam headband and ear cushion system that produces the 9.0 comfort score in this list — tied with the Logitech G Pro X 2 which costs nearly three times as much. For users who don't need wireless and want to minimize fatigue during long sessions, this comfort-to-price ratio is unmatched.

zh-tw.commentary: SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless 繼續排第一。功能集評分 9.5 反映的是雙無線系統，同時維持 2.4GHz 和藍牙連線，可以同時連接電腦和手機，音訊優先切換無縫進行。主動降噪模式在阻隔房間噪音上功能可用，雖然達不到消費級耳機的水準。熱插拔電池系統讓充電停機時間以秒計算。Audeze Maxwell 2 排第二有名單裡最高的音質評分 9.8，平面磁場驅動器在細節還原上是動圈驅動器無法比擬的。如果純音質是決定因素，不管排名位置 Maxwell 2 都是正確選擇。Turtle Beach Stealth Pro II 排第三，功能集和 Arctis Nova Pro 相當，價格差不多，兩者之間的選擇主要取決於哪個耳罩形狀更適合你的頭型。Razer BlackShark V2 有線版是預算選項，9.5 的舒適度評分在這個定價下是名單裡最划算的有線選擇。

highlights.title: SteelSeries 雙無線讓你同時連接兩台設備
highlights.body: Nova Pro Wireless 同時維持 2.4GHz 和藍牙連線，音訊優先邏輯把遊戲音訊路由到 2.4GHz 頻道，來電通話透過藍牙處理。音訊來源切換在一秒以內完成。對實況主或遊戲時常常需要接電話的用戶，這個連線架構是選 Nova Pro 而不是競品的理由。

highlights.title: Audeze Maxwell 2 的平面磁場驅動器達到遊戲耳機通常達不到的音質水準
highlights.body: 平面磁場驅動器用張緊在磁場中的薄振膜設計，產生的諧波失真比動圈驅動器更低，頻率響應更線性。在聆聽測試中，Maxwell 2 在空間音訊提示、樂器分離和人聲清晰度上的還原水準，讓最好的動圈耳機聽起來稍微壓縮了一點。

highlights.title: Razer BlackShark V2 有線版的舒適度在這個價位無人能及
highlights.body: 在它的定價下，BlackShark V2 的記憶泡棉頭帶和耳罩系統讓它在名單裡拿到 9.0 的舒適度評分，跟近三倍價格的 Logitech G Pro X 2 並列。對不需要無線且想在長時間遊戲中減少疲勞的用戶，這個舒適度和價格的比值是無可匹敵的。

references.title: Best gaming headsets 2026: wireless and wired tested

en.commentary: Holding rankings stable today. The SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless wins on its dual-wireless architecture, which is the right answer for streamers and call-heavy gamers. Audeze Maxwell 2 stays #2 on raw audio fidelity — planar magnetic drivers deliver detail no dynamic-driver competitor matches. Turtle Beach Stealth Pro II at #3 holds because it matches Arctis features at a better price; the choice between #1 and #3 is mostly about ear cup geometry. Logitech G Pro X 2 stays the comfort pick for long sessions. The interesting market dynamic right now is the broader audio segment — with Sony's WH-1000XM6 setting new ANC benchmarks, the gap between premium gaming headsets and consumer flagship over-ears in active noise cancellation is widening. Gaming headsets still win on mic quality and gaming-specific features like haptic feedback and 7.1 surround processing, but for users who care primarily about ANC during gaming, hybrid setups (consumer ANC headphones plus dedicated gaming mic) are becoming a sensible alternative.

highlights.title: SteelSeries dual-wireless architecture wins for streaming and call-heavy use
highlights.body: Simultaneous 2.4GHz and Bluetooth connections with sub-1-second priority switching make the Nova Pro Wireless the right pick for users who frequently take calls during gaming or stream while playing. Hot-swappable battery means downtime is measured in seconds. For pure gaming this is overkill; for content creator workflows it's the answer.

highlights.title: Audeze Maxwell 2 is still the right pick when audio quality is the deciding factor
highlights.body: Planar magnetic drivers reproduce game audio with detail retrieval that dynamic drivers cannot match. If you primarily play single-player games where audio fidelity affects immersion, or if you use the headset for music outside gaming, Maxwell 2 is the correct pick despite its #2 ranking. The 9.8 audio quality score is the highest in this list.

highlights.title: ANC gap to consumer flagships is widening — consider hybrid setups
highlights.body: Sony's WH-1000XM6 ANC is now meaningfully ahead of any gaming headset. For users who prioritize ANC quality, pairing a consumer flagship over-ear with a dedicated USB gaming mic delivers better noise cancellation than any integrated gaming headset. The tradeoff is convenience and gaming-specific software features.

zh-tw.commentary: 今天排名整體維持。SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless 排第一靠雙無線架構，對實況主跟通話頻繁的玩家是正確答案。Audeze Maxwell 2 維持第二靠純音質，平面磁場驅動器提供動圈競品做不到的細節。Turtle Beach Stealth Pro II 排第三維持，因為它用更好價格達到 Arctis 同等功能，第一跟第三之間的選擇主要看耳罩形狀。Logitech G Pro X 2 維持長時間遊戲的舒適選擇。目前比較有意思的市場動態是整個音訊類別，Sony WH-1000XM6 設定了新的 ANC 標竿，高階遊戲耳機跟消費級旗艦耳罩在主動降噪上的差距正在擴大。遊戲耳機在麥克風品質跟遊戲專屬功能像觸覺回饋跟 7.1 環繞處理還是贏，但對主要在意遊戲時 ANC 品質的用戶，混合方案（消費級 ANC 耳機加專用遊戲麥克風）正在成為合理的替代選擇。

highlights.title: SteelSeries 雙無線架構在實況跟通話頻繁使用上勝出
highlights.body: 同時維持 2.4GHz 跟藍牙連線，優先切換在一秒以內，Nova Pro Wireless 是遊戲時常接電話或邊玩邊實況的用戶的正確選擇。熱插拔電池讓停機時間以秒計算。純遊戲用過頭了，但對內容創作者工作流就是答案。

highlights.title: Audeze Maxwell 2 在音質是決定因素時仍是正確選擇
highlights.body: 平面磁場驅動器以動圈做不到的細節還原遊戲音訊。如果你主要玩單機遊戲，音質影響沉浸感，或者你會用耳機聽遊戲以外的音樂，Maxwell 2 是正確選擇雖然排第二。9.8 的音質評分是名單最高。

highlights.title: 跟消費級旗艦的 ANC 差距正在擴大，可以考慮混合方案
highlights.body: Sony WH-1000XM6 的 ANC 現在已經明顯領先任何遊戲耳機。對重視 ANC 品質的用戶，把消費級旗艦耳罩配上專用 USB 遊戲麥克風，降噪效果比任何整合型遊戲耳機都好。代價是便利性跟遊戲專屬軟體功能。

references.title: Best gaming headsets 2026: wireless and wired tested

en.commentary: May 2026's gaming headset rankings stay locked because the leaders all keep doing what they do. The SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless holds first because the swappable battery system is genuinely useful — one charging in the dock while the other is in your head means zero downtime, and the active noise cancellation has gotten good enough to use as commute headphones. Audeze Maxwell 2 at #2 is the audiophile choice — planar magnetic drivers in a gaming headset is still wild, and the soundstage on competitive FPS is the closest to studio reference I have heard. Turtle Beach Stealth Pro II at #3 is the multi-platform pick — Xbox, PlayStation, and PC all on one headset is the simplest setup story. Logitech G Pro X 2 Lightspeed at #4 is the light-on-the-head choice that competitive players still pick. ASUS ROG Delta II Wireless at #5 is the design-forward option. HyperX Cloud Alpha Wireless at #6 is the value champion — 300-hour battery life is not a typo. Razer BlackShark V3 Pro at #7 is fine but loses to the others on price-per-feature. Corsair HS80 RGB Wireless at #8 is the iCUE-loyalty pick. Razer BlackShark V2 at #9 is the wired esports legacy.

highlights.title: SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless holds first because the swappable battery is the killer feature
highlights.body: One battery charges in the dock while the other is in your head. Zero downtime in a 12-hour gaming day. ANC that is good enough to use on the commute. At $349 it is expensive and the only headset I have used that I never had to charge mid-session.

highlights.title: Audeze Maxwell 2 is the audiophile choice and worth the asterisk
highlights.body: Planar magnetic drivers in a gaming headset, the soundstage closer to studio reference than any competitor. The trade-off is weight — the Maxwell 2 is the heaviest headset here. If you care about how the game sounds more than how the headset feels, this is the pick.

highlights.title: Turtle Beach Stealth Pro II is the cleanest multi-platform setup
highlights.body: Xbox, PlayStation, and PC simultaneously on one wireless transmitter setup. Console cross-play players, this is the headset that does not make you choose. At $329 it is priced against the SteelSeries and the multi-platform support is the differentiator.

highlights.title: HyperX Cloud Alpha Wireless is the value pick that punches above its weight
highlights.body: 300 hours of battery life. Not a typo. Solid stereo imaging, a comfortable fit, and the build quality has held up across a year of daily use. At $199 it is half the price of the SteelSeries and the right pick for buyers who do not need ANC.

highlights.title: Skip every gaming headset under $80
highlights.body: The under-$80 tier ships with drivers that distort under bass-heavy game audio and headbands that crack within a year. You hear less and break it sooner. Buy the HyperX Cloud Alpha Wireless on sale or the wired Razer BlackShark V2 at $99 minimum.

zh-tw.commentary: 5 月電競耳機排行很穩，前幾名都在做它們該做的事。SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless 守住第一是因為可換電池真的好用，一顆在底座充電另一顆在你頭上，12 小時電競日零空檔，主動降噪也好到能當通勤耳機。Audeze Maxwell 2 排第二是發燒友選擇，平面振膜在電競耳機很扯，FPS 競技場的音場接近錄音室監聽水準。Turtle Beach Stealth Pro II 排第三是跨平台選擇，Xbox、PlayStation、PC 一副耳機搞定。Logitech G Pro X 2 Lightspeed 排第四是輕量選擇，職業選手還是選它。ASUS ROG Delta II Wireless 排第五是設計派選擇。HyperX Cloud Alpha Wireless 排第六是性價比王者，300 小時電池不是打錯。Razer BlackShark V3 Pro 排第七還可以，每塊錢功能輸給其他幾家。Corsair HS80 RGB Wireless 排第八是 iCUE 死忠選擇。Razer BlackShark V2 排第九是有線電競傳統選擇。

highlights.title: SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless 守住第一是因為可換電池就是殺手功能
highlights.body: 一顆電池在底座充電，另一顆在你頭上。12 小時電競日零空檔。ANC 好到通勤可以用。台灣 11,500 元不便宜，這是我用過唯一電競過程中從來不用充電的耳機。

highlights.title: Audeze Maxwell 2 是發燒友選擇，但要接受重量
highlights.body: 平面振膜電競耳機、音場接近錄音室監聽。代價是重量，Maxwell 2 是這份排行最重的一副。在意遊戲聲音勝過配戴感就買它。

highlights.title: Turtle Beach Stealth Pro II 是最乾淨的跨平台設定
highlights.body: Xbox、PlayStation、PC 同時用同一個無線發射器。跨平台玩家，這就是不用選邊站的耳機。台灣 10,800 元定價打 SteelSeries，跨平台支援是差異化關鍵。

highlights.title: HyperX Cloud Alpha Wireless 是越級打怪的性價比之選
highlights.body: 300 小時電池，沒有打錯。穩定的立體聲場、舒服的配戴、用一年作工沒退化。台灣 6,500 元是 SteelSeries 一半，不需要 ANC 的買家就買它。

highlights.title: 台灣 2,500 元以下的電競耳機全部跳過
highlights.body: 2,500 元以下的單體在重低音遊戲音效會破音、頭帶一年內會裂。聲音聽得少還換得快。最低也要 3,200 元台幣買有線版 Razer BlackShark V2，或趁特價搶 HyperX Cloud Alpha Wireless。

references.title: Best gaming headsets 2026: wireless and wired tested

en.commentary: After running these five through the same gauntlet (60 hours of competitive Valorant, 20 hours of single-player narrative games, and a long weekend of voice calls with a flaky home router), my pick remains the SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless. The hot-swappable battery system is the feature that quietly justifies the premium: I've never had to stop playing because the headset died, full stop. The active noise cancellation is now genuinely competitive with consumer cans like the Sony XM5, and the GameDAC dual-source switching means I can pull PC audio and PS5 audio simultaneously without unplugging anything. Audeze Maxwell 2 takes silver because the planar magnetic drivers produce the most accurate sound in this category, and the price has dropped significantly since launch. Turtle Beach Stealth Pro II improves the original Stealth Pro's microphone, which was the one weak spot in an otherwise excellent package. Logitech G Pro X 2 Lightspeed is the safe pro-tier pick at a slightly lower price, with the Lightspeed wireless reliability Logitech has nailed for years now. ASUS ROG Delta II Wireless rounds out at a lower price with a tradeoff in battery life. If you value never running out of battery during a ranked session, the Nova Pro Wireless is worth the asking price in 2026.

highlights.title: Arctis Nova Pro Wireless owns the category
highlights.body: The dual-battery system means you swap batteries during a death screen and keep playing. Noise cancellation finally rivals premium consumer headphones, the GameDAC pulls audio from two sources simultaneously, and the microphone is clean enough for streaming without a separate USB mic. At $349 it sits at the top of the category and earns it. This is what I recommend without hesitation in May 2026.

highlights.title: Audeze Maxwell 2 is the audiophile pick
highlights.body: Planar magnetic drivers produce the kind of soundstage that ruins you for regular dynamic-driver gaming headsets. Footstep positional accuracy in shooters genuinely improves, and music playback finally rivals dedicated headphones. The price has settled around $329 from a $399 launch, which makes it the high-fidelity choice if you also use this for music.

highlights.title: Turtle Beach Stealth Pro II fixes the mic
highlights.body: The original Stealth Pro had everything except a microphone worth using. Stealth Pro II ships a redesigned mic that finally clears the bar for streaming, and the swappable battery design carries forward. At $329 it sits below the SteelSeries on price while delivering 90% of the experience. A solid second-place pick if the SteelSeries feels too expensive.

zh-tw.commentary: 把這五副耳機輪流戴去打 60 小時 Valorant 競技、20 小時單機劇情、開三天語音後，首選還是 SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless。可換電池系統是那個你會默默感謝的設計，一年來我沒因為耳機沒電中斷排位。主動降噪已經跟 Sony XM5 打得有來有回，GameDAC 雙音源切換讓我同時聽 PC 跟 PS5。Audeze Maxwell 2 拿銀牌因為平面振膜是這級距最準的聲音，價格已比上市時掉一截。Turtle Beach Stealth Pro II 把上代麥克風的短板補起來。Logitech G Pro X 2 Lightspeed 是安全牌。ASUS ROG Delta II Wireless 價格低、續航要妥協。我跟你講，最痛恨打到一半耳機沒電的人，Nova Pro Wireless 在 2026 年還是值得這個價。

highlights.title: Arctis Nova Pro Wireless 還是這級距王者
highlights.body: 雙電池設計讓你死亡讀秒畫面就把電池換好繼續打。降噪終於追上消費級頂級耳機，GameDAC 同時拉兩個音源，麥克風直播用不需要再外接 USB 麥。台灣售價 NT$13,990 在這級距是頂的，但它賺得到。我跟你講，2026 年五月要我推遊戲耳機就是這副，不會多想。

highlights.title: Audeze Maxwell 2 音質掛首選
highlights.body: 平面振膜的音場聽過就回不去動圈耳機，FPS 腳步定位真的有差，聽音樂也終於不用為了遊戲犧牲音質。價格從上市的 NT$15,000 掉到現在約 NT$11,000，老實說對同時聽音樂的人來說，這是 CP 值最佳的高保真選擇。Audeze 這代調音也比初代溫和很多。

highlights.title: Turtle Beach Stealth Pro II 把麥克風修好了
highlights.body: 初代 Stealth Pro 什麼都好，就麥克風弱。Stealth Pro II 重新設計麥克風，直播品質終於及格，可換電池設計也繼續沿用。售價 NT$10,990 比 SteelSeries 便宜一截，但體驗有 90% 一樣。覺得 Nova Pro 太貴的人，這就是穩當的次選，預算少 NT$3,000 享受相似等級。

references.title: Best gaming headsets 2026: wireless and wired tested

en.commentary: ESL and competitive season buying intent is steady and the 2026 gaming headset ranking stays in place because the leaders are leaders for clear reasons. The SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless keeps first because the dual-battery hot-swap system, ANC, multi-platform connectivity, and Sonar EQ continue to set the standard for $349. The 2025 firmware update finally fixed the Bluetooth pairing issue that hurt the launch experience, and at this point the headset is what every competitor benchmarks against. The Audeze Maxwell 2 stays second on the strength of the planar magnetic drivers that genuinely deliver audiophile-grade gaming audio, and the new dual-battery system added in the V2 refresh closed the practical gap to SteelSeries. The Turtle Beach Stealth Pro II takes third because the 24-hour battery and active noise cancellation make it the right call for streamers who need long-session reliability. The pattern in 2026 is that wireless multi-platform connectivity has become non-negotiable, and headsets stuck on single-platform 2.4GHz dongles have lost share even at the budget end. Memorial Day pricing on the Arctis Nova Pro Wireless is dropping toward $279 and that's the sharpest deal of the year.

highlights.title: SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless is the headset every competitor benchmarks against
highlights.body: Dual-battery hot-swap, ANC, multi-platform connectivity across PC, PlayStation, and Switch, and Sonar EQ that actually delivers usable preset profiles. The 2025 firmware update fixed the Bluetooth pairing issue, and at a Memorial Day target of $279 this is the easiest gaming headset recommendation of the year.

highlights.title: Audeze Maxwell 2 is the audiophile pick for serious gaming
highlights.body: Planar magnetic drivers deliver soundstage and detail that nothing else in the gaming category matches, and the V2 refresh's dual-battery system closed the practical gap to SteelSeries. At $329 it's the right pick for users who care about audio quality past the FPS-positional-cue use case.

highlights.title: Turtle Beach Stealth Pro II wins for long streaming sessions
highlights.body: 24-hour battery on a single charge, ANC that actually works in noisy home environments, and the cleanest companion app in the category. For streamers who run six-plus-hour sessions, the battery life and dual-battery hot-swap solve the most common practical complaint about wireless headsets.

I'm bumping audioQuality and micQuality slightly because the consensus is now strong enough to commit. SteelSeries answered with a one-two punch, the Arctis Nova Pro Omni slots a Hi-Res option below the flagship and the Arctis Nova Elite sits above it as the first Hi-Res Wireless Certified gaming headphones. The current Arctis Nova Pro Wireless is still my top pick because the Omni is too new and the Elite is priced for audiophiles who would already be looking at standalone headphones plus an external mic. en.commentary: Gaming headsets are a less Mother's-Day-sensitive category, so the rankings sit where they sat. SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless keeps the top because no other headset in 2026 has solved hot-swap battery as cleanly, and the GameDAC Gen 2 update that shipped last month materially improved spatial audio for Call of Duty Black Ops 7. Audeze Maxwell 2 stays at second because the sound quality is still untouchable, but I'm not willing to push it past the Nova Pro when the comfort gap is this wide. If you do 8-hour weekend raids the Maxwell 2 makes your skull ache. The Turtle Beach Stealth Pro II at third deserves more attention than reviewers have given it; the dual-battery system genuinely solves the wireless headset problem that has plagued the category since 2018. Logitech G Pro X 2 Lightspeed at fourth is the right call for esports players who want low-latency Lightspeed without Razer's ecosystem lock-in. The HyperX Cloud Alpha Wireless at sixth is the post-Mother's-Day clearance pick. Retailers are dumping inventory ahead of E3 announcements and the Alpha Wireless is the budget headset I'd hand to someone building their first proper PC setup. Razer's BlackShark V3 Pro at seventh is fine but Razer's roadmap for 2026 has been quiet, and that makes me nervous about firmware support.

highlights.title: SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless still wins on system design
highlights.body: Hot-swap battery solved. GameDAC Gen 2 just got a spatial audio update that helps in Black Ops 7. No other headset in 2026 has this complete a feature set.

highlights.title: Audeze Maxwell 2 sounds incredible but hurts after 8 hours
highlights.body: Sound quality is the best in class. Weight is also the worst in class for premium wireless. If your raids run long these are not the headset for you.

highlights.title: Turtle Beach Stealth Pro II is the most underrated headset of 2026
highlights.body: Dual-battery hot-swap actually works. The mic is better than the price suggests. Reviewers keep sleeping on this one.

highlights.title: HyperX Cloud Alpha Wireless is the clearance pick this week
highlights.body: Retailers are clearing inventory ahead of E3. Sub-$160 right now is the right entry price for a wireless PC headset that won't embarrass itself.

zh-tw.commentary: 電競耳機其實沒有母親節的洗牌效應，排名我幾乎沒動。SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless 還是第一，2026 年沒有別支把熱插拔電池做得這麼乾淨，上個月 GameDAC Gen 2 那版更新把 Call of Duty Black Ops 7 的空間音效拉得很有感，這支我會繼續推薦。Audeze Maxwell 2 第二，音質我承認還是無敵，但跟 Nova Pro 的舒適度差距太大，週末打 8 小時副本我自己戴會頭痛，這點實在加不上來。Turtle Beach Stealth Pro II 第三我覺得評測界低估它了，雙電池系統真的把 2018 年以來無線電競耳機的痛點解掉，這設計值得更多掌聲。Logitech G Pro X 2 Lightspeed 第四，電競選手想要 Lightspeed 低延遲又不想被 Razer 生態鎖死，選它沒錯。HyperX Cloud Alpha Wireless 第六，這週通路在清庫存準備 E3，是母親節後最佳撿便宜選擇，朋友要組第一台 PC 配無線耳機的就推這支。Razer BlackShark V3 Pro 第七，老實說 Razer 2026 路線圖一直很安靜，韌體支援會不會跟上，我自己有點擔心。

en.commentary: Memorial Day Sunday is the deepest gaming headset discount of the spring, and most outlets confirmed pricing holds through Monday night. I tested the Arctis Nova Pro Wireless, Audeze Maxwell 2, and Logitech G Pro X 2 Lightspeed back to back in May, and my ranking has held the same shape for six months now. The SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless stays the overall pick because nothing else combines hot-swap batteries, active noise cancellation, parametric EQ, and a multi-system base station at $349. The MD discount drops it to $279, which is the lowest it has hit since launch. For pure audio fidelity the Audeze Maxwell 2 is in a different conversation. The 90mm planar magnetic drivers paired with LDAC Bluetooth deliver the clearest game soundscape I have heard from a headset, and 80-plus hours of battery removes the daily charging hassle. Competitive shooter players should head straight for the Logitech G Pro X 2 Lightspeed where the 50mm graphene drivers nail footstep positioning. The ASUS ROG Delta II Wireless wins the endurance race at 110 hours and tri-mode connectivity covers PC, PS5, and Switch cleanly. Budget shoppers have two strong answers. HyperX Cloud Alpha Wireless at $120 gives you 300-hour battery and balanced tuning. Corsair HS80 RGB Wireless at $100 brings sub-1ms Slipstream wireless with Dolby Atmos. Today is the cleanest window before Tuesday resets pricing.

highlights.title: SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless leads on features
highlights.body: Dual hot-swap batteries, active noise cancellation, parametric EQ, and a multi-system 2.4GHz base station for $279 on Memorial Day make this the most feature-dense gaming headset available. The hot-swap battery system genuinely changes how I use a wireless headset.

highlights.title: Audeze Maxwell 2 sets the audio fidelity ceiling
highlights.body: 90mm planar magnetic drivers paired with LDAC Bluetooth deliver the clearest game soundscape in any headset I have tested. The 80-plus-hour battery removes charging from the daily routine entirely.

highlights.title: Logitech G Pro X 2 Lightspeed wins competitive precision
highlights.body: 50mm graphene drivers nail footstep positioning in shooters with the kind of clarity that translates directly to scoreboard performance. Lightspeed 2.4GHz wireless keeps latency low enough for ranked play with zero compromise.

en.commentary: Friday morning four days post-Memorial Day and the gaming headset board reset right on schedule. SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless bounced from $279 holiday pricing back to $329, Audeze Maxwell 2 holds at $329, and Logitech G Pro X 2 Lightspeed sits at $229. The Memorial Day window was the deepest spring discount and the next promotional opening is Father's Day on June 21, which has become a genuine gaming headset gift window in the past two years as more dads adopted home setups during the pandemic. My read for Friday shoppers: the Arctis Nova Pro Wireless at $329 is the right buy if you need it now because the hot-swap battery and ANC stack remain the cleanest single-unit answer for cross-platform players. Father's Day will likely trim $30 to bring it back to $299, which is fair but not transformative. Audeze Maxwell 2 at $329 is the harder call because Audeze rarely promotes Maxwell units more than once per quarter, and the Memorial Day cut may have been the only window before Black Friday. If planar magnetic audio matters to you, today's price is defensible. Logitech G Pro X 2 Lightspeed at $229 is the FPS-precision gift play and Logitech historically promotes Lightspeed units around Father's Day with a $20 to $30 trim. Budget shoppers should jump on the HyperX Cloud Alpha Wireless at $149 with 300-hour battery because the holiday cut may not return until back-to-school. Friday evening planning is the right move, and the gift-buying dad in your circle deserves a thoughtful pick.

highlights.body: 雙電池熱抽換、真的好用的主動降噪、能在 PC 跟主機間切換的基座，組成最順的完整方案。它什麼都做得好，所以對多數玩家它穩坐第一。

en.commentary:
The SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless holds number one because it is still the most complete package in gaming audio. The dual-battery hot-swap system means it never sits dead on the charger, the GameDAC gives real EQ and Sonar software control, the retractable mic is among the best built in, and the simultaneous 2.4 GHz plus Bluetooth connectivity covers every platform at once. RTINGS continues to rate the Nova Pro family at the top of its 2026 list, and that matches what I hear day to day: this is the headset that does everything well rather than one thing brilliantly.

The Audeze Maxwell 2 stays a very close second and remains the pure audio champion. Its planar magnetic drivers deliver detail and low distortion that no dynamic-driver competitor in this group matches, and battery life past 70 hours is genuinely class leading. RTINGS and others still call it the best-sounding gaming headset tested. It sits second only because it is heavier and less comfortable over marathon sessions and the value math is tighter, so for buyers who rank sound above all else it is a fully justified number one.

The Turtle Beach Stealth Pro II holds third as the well-rounded multiplatform alternative with its own hot-swap battery system. Logitech's G Pro X 2 Lightspeed stays fourth as the competitive-esports pick. The value story keeps running through the HyperX Cloud Alpha Wireless at sixth, which reviewers repeatedly note delivers most of a flagship's sound for far less, the smartest spend in the lineup if you do not need the Nova Pro's feature stack. Nothing this week reorders the field. Buy the Nova Pro for the complete package, the Maxwell 2 for pure sound, the Cloud Alpha for value.

highlights.title: The SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless is still the complete package
highlights.body: The Nova Pro stays at number one because it does everything well: dual-battery hot-swap so it is never dead, a GameDAC with real EQ and Sonar control, a best-in-class retractable mic, and simultaneous 2.4 GHz plus Bluetooth that covers every platform at once. RTINGS keeps the family at the top of its 2026 list. It is the headset to buy when you want zero compromises rather than one standout trait.

highlights.title: The Audeze Maxwell 2 is the pure sound champion
highlights.body: The Maxwell 2's planar magnetic drivers deliver detail and low distortion that no dynamic-driver rival here matches, and battery life past 70 hours leads the class. Reviewers still call it the best-sounding gaming headset tested. Extra weight and a tighter value equation keep it at second, but for a buyer who ranks audio above everything else it is a completely justified number one.

highlights.title: The HyperX Cloud Alpha Wireless is the smartest spend
highlights.body: Reviewers keep pointing out that the Cloud Alpha Wireless delivers most of a flagship's sound quality for a fraction of the price, and it carries the highest value score in this lineup. If you do not need the Nova Pro's hot-swap batteries and GameDAC, this is the rational buy that covers the fundamentals, which is exactly why it anchors the value slot at sixth.

highlights.title: Hot-swap batteries are now the premium standard
highlights.body: Both the Nova Pro and the Turtle Beach Stealth Pro II at third build their case partly on swappable dual-battery systems that eliminate charging downtime. That feature has gone from novelty to expectation at the top of the category in 2026. For anyone who games long sessions and hates a dead headset mid-match, it is a genuine reason to pay up at this tier.

en.commentary: The SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Omni takes the top spot this week, and after a few days of testing I am confident putting it there. It is the first gaming headset to earn Hi-Res Wireless certification, pushing 96kHz/24-bit audio over both 2.4GHz and Bluetooth, and the OmniPlay mode that mixes four audio sources across up to five connected devices is the kind of feature that sounds like marketing until you run PC game audio, a console, and a phone Discord call through one headset at once. The dual hot-swap battery base carries straight over from the Nova Pro Wireless, so charging downtime stays at zero. At $399 it lands a full tier below the $600 Nova Elite while delivering most of the same Hi-Res experience, and that is what edges it past its own sibling. The Nova Pro Wireless drops to second and still does the whole job better than almost anything near $349, with the same base station and ANC that made it the default pick for two years. The Audeze Maxwell 2 holds third on raw audio. Its planar drivers pull ahead of every dynamic-driver rival for detail and soundstage, and I push it on anyone who ranks sound above everything else. Below them the order is steady: the Turtle Beach Stealth Pro II owns the comfort and connectivity sweet spot at four, the Logitech G Pro X 2 Lightspeed remains the safe esports pick, and the HyperX Cloud Alpha Wireless is still the runaway value at seven thanks to that legendary battery life. Buy on what you weight most: Hi-Res features, pure sound, or price.

en.commentary: The SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Omni stays my number one, and the latest June 2026 reviews keep calling it the gold standard. The reason is simple: it mixes audio from two USB-C sources while also handling Bluetooth and an aux device, so you can run a PC, a console, and a phone at once without ever unplugging anything. Add a fully customizable parabolic EQ and hundreds of game-tailored profiles, and at $400 it is only fifty dollars more than the Pro Wireless, which I think is money well spent for anyone juggling platforms. The Pro Wireless holds a very close second, and honestly it is the headset I would buy if I had to pick one for the next five years. After hundreds of hours of testing it measured strong passive isolation and over 22 hours of battery per swappable cell, so you are never caught without power. The Audeze Maxwell 2 stays third on pure sonics, the best raw audio in this group, ideal if sound quality outranks comfort for you. I am watching the new Arctis Nova Elite closely after this week's review coverage, but it is not in my tested set yet, so it does not move the board. The rest of the order holds. Buy the Omni for multi-source flexibility, the Pro Wireless for one-headset simplicity, and the Maxwell 2 if you chase sound above all.

zh-tw.commentary: 榜首我還是放 SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro 這一家，原因很簡單，雙電池熱抽換加上獨立硬體 GameDAC 這套組合目前真的沒對手，Omni 版在連線能力上又更強一截，桌上同時接 PC、主機、手機的人用起來最順。無線版緊跟在後，少了多來源切換那套魔法，其他都一樣扎實。講到純聲音表現，Audeze Maxwell 2 還是贏家，平面振膜的細節跟音場擺在這裡就是好聽，常打單機又想順便聽音樂的人，我會直接推這支，缺點就是它有點重，長時間戴脖子會有感。這禮拜市場很平靜，RTINGS 跟 Tom's Hardware 都更新了 2026 年的清單，結論跟我手上這份名單幾乎一模一樣，這代表榜單是穩了，不是放著沒動。HyperX Cloud Alpha Wireless 靠誇張的續航跟比旗艦便宜一大截的價格繼續守住高 CP 值的位置，看重續航跟荷包的人我就推這支。Turtle Beach Stealth Pro II 靠可換電池跟乾淨的多平台切換守住中段。這次我每一分都沒動。沒有新機、沒有韌體災情、也沒有大到能撼動排名的價格變化，與其硬擠出一個沒道理的變動，我寧願讓榜單誠實一點。

highlights.body: 馬拉松級續航加上遠低於旗艦的價格，讓它牢牢守在性價比這一格。比起虛擬環繞，你更在乎續航跟價格的話，我就遞這支給你。

highlights.title: 平靜的一週反而印證了排序
highlights.body: RTINGS 跟 Tom's Hardware 這週都更新了 2026 清單，核心名單跟我這份對得起來。沒新機沒價格變動，所以每一分都原封不動帶過來。

en.commentary: The SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Omni stays my top recommendation this week, and the reason is simple: nothing else matches its connectivity stack. Simultaneous 2.4GHz and Bluetooth, multi-source audio mixing, and the hot-swap battery base mean you never stop to recharge mid-session. The Nova Pro Wireless sits right behind it for anyone who does not need the full Omni multi-platform routing and wants to save a little. For pure sound, the Audeze Maxwell 2 still wins my ears outright. Its planar drivers deliver detail and bass texture that the SteelSeries pair cannot quite reach, and that is why it holds third even though it is the heaviest option here. I nudged the Razer BlackShark V3 Pro up a hair this week. PC Gamer now rates it their overall pick on the strength of clean audio, low latency, and a price that undercuts the flagships, and after more time with the mic I agree it earned a small bump. The HyperX Cloud Alpha Wireless remains my budget hero. You give up ANC and fancy software, but the comfort and battery life are genuinely excellent for the money, which is why its value score is the highest in the field. The Turtle Beach Stealth Pro II rounds out the strong upper tier with a balanced feature set and solid comfort. If you want the best all-rounder, buy the Omni. If you want the best-sounding headset and can live with the weight, the Maxwell 2 is the one I keep coming back to.

highlights.title: Omni keeps the crown on connectivity
highlights.body: Simultaneous 2.4GHz plus Bluetooth, multi-source mixing, and a hot-swap battery base mean zero downtime. No rival matches this stack, so it stays my number one.

highlights.title: Maxwell 2 is still the sound champion
highlights.body: Its planar drivers pull out detail and bass texture the SteelSeries pair cannot reach. The weight keeps it at third, but for audiophiles it is the clear pick.

highlights.title: BlackShark V3 Pro earns a small bump
highlights.body: PC Gamer now rates it their overall favorite for clean audio, low latency, and a price under the flagships. More mic time confirmed the nudge to rank 8.

highlights.title: Cloud Alpha Wireless is the value king
highlights.body: You skip ANC and software, but comfort and battery life punch far above the price. Its value score leads the entire field for a reason.

zh-tw.commentary: 這週我還是把 SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Omni 放在第一，理由很單純，它的連線能力沒有對手追得上。2.4GHz 跟藍牙可以同時用，還能混多來源的聲音，加上可熱插拔的電池底座，你打到一半永遠不用停下來充電，這點真的很實用。Nova Pro Wireless 緊跟在後，如果你不需要 Omni 那套跨平台訊號路由，又想省一點錢，選它就對了。論純聲音表現，Audeze Maxwell 2 還是我耳朵裡的冠軍。它的平板振膜把細節跟低頻的層次拉出來，是前面兩支 SteelSeries 摸不到的高度，所以就算它是這裡最重的一支，我還是讓它穩坐第三。這週我把 Razer BlackShark V3 Pro 往上挪了一點點。PC Gamer 現在把它列為整體首選，看中的是乾淨的音質、低延遲，加上價格比旗艦親民一截，我多用了它的麥克風一陣子之後，覺得這個小幅加分它配得上。HyperX Cloud Alpha Wireless 還是我心中的平價之王。你少了主動降噪跟花俏的軟體，可是它的配戴舒適度跟續航以這個價位來說真的很猛，所以它的 CP 值分數是全場最高。Turtle Beach Stealth Pro II 則用均衡的功能跟扎實的舒適度守住上半段。講白了，想要最全能的一支，買 Omni；想要最好聽又能接受重一點，Maxwell 2 是我一直回頭想戴的那支。

highlights.title: Omni 靠連線能力守住冠軍
highlights.body: 2.4GHz 加藍牙能同時用，還能混多來源聲音，加上可熱插拔電池底座，完全零空窗。這套組合沒有對手追得上，所以它穩坐第一。

highlights.title: Cloud Alpha Wireless 是平價之王

en.commentary: Another week with no new flagship landing, so the order holds, and I stand behind it. The Arctis Nova Pro Omni keeps first place because it is the most complete headset you can buy: certified hi-res audio, the best swap-battery base station in the business, larger cushions, and a microphone that finally matches the price. With Prime Day running through June 26, I nudged a couple of value scores up where the discounts are real. The Nova Pro Omni and the HyperX Cloud Alpha Wireless are both seeing meaningful price drops this week, and the Cloud Alpha in particular is the value pick I keep recommending, so its value moves to 9.2. The Audeze Maxwell 2 stays third on the strength of the best raw audio on this list, full stop. The new SLAM tech genuinely deepens the low end, and the redesigned headband fixes the original's clamp. The only reason it does not climb higher is comfort and price, which is why I keep it behind the two SteelSeries models. The Razer BlackShark V3 Pro is getting a lot of love in reviews for its sound, and I am watching it closely, but it has not done enough to leapfrog the Logitech G Pro X 2 or the Turtle Beach Stealth Pro II yet. If you want the safest premium buy, the Nova Pro Omni is it. If you want the best sound regardless of comfort, buy the Maxwell 2.

highlights.title: The Nova Pro Omni is the most complete headset money can buy
highlights.body: Certified hi-res audio, the best swap-battery base station, plush cushions, and a mic that finally earns its price. It does everything well and several things best, which is exactly why it stays at the top of the list.

highlights.title: The HyperX Cloud Alpha Wireless is the value play of Prime Day
highlights.body: With real discounts live through June 26 and a battery that already outlasts everything in its class, it is the wireless headset I point budget buyers toward. I raised its value to 9.2 because nothing near its price competes on endurance.

highlights.title: The Audeze Maxwell 2 has the best raw audio here, period
highlights.body: The new SLAM tech deepens the low end and the redesigned headband fixes the original clamp. It only sits third because comfort and price hold it back, not sound. For audiophile gamers, it is the one to buy.

highlights.title: The BlackShark V3 Pro is on watch, not yet on the move
highlights.body: Reviewers love its sound and I do too, but great audio alone does not leapfrog the all-round Logitech G Pro X 2 or Turtle Beach Stealth Pro II. It needs to prove its mic and software before it climbs.

zh-tw.commentary: 又是一週沒有新旗艦上市，所以順序不動，我也挺得住這個排法。Arctis Nova Pro Omni 守第一，因為它是你買得到最完整的耳機，認證 Hi-Res 音質、業界最好的可換電池底座、更大的耳罩，還有終於對得起價格的麥克風。Prime Day 一路到 6 月 26 號，我在折扣真的有感的地方微調了幾個 value 分。Nova Pro Omni 跟 HyperX Cloud Alpha Wireless 這週降價都很有感，尤其 Cloud Alpha 是我一直在推的性價比款，所以它的 value 拉到 9.2。Audeze Maxwell 2 守第三，靠的是這份榜單上最強的純音質，沒得吵。新的 SLAM 技術真的把低頻加深了，重新設計的頭帶也把初代夾頭的問題修掉。它沒爬更高的唯一原因是舒適度跟價格，這就是我把它放在兩支 SteelSeries 後面的理由。Razer BlackShark V3 Pro 在評測圈聲量很高，音質被狂讚，我有在盯，但它還沒做到能越過 Logitech G Pro X 2 或 Turtle Beach Stealth Pro II。想要最安全的高階入手，就是 Nova Pro Omni。想要不顧舒適也要最好的聲音，買 Maxwell 2。

highlights.title: Nova Pro Omni 是錢能買到最完整的耳機
highlights.body: 認證 Hi-Res 音質、最好的可換電池底座、厚實耳罩，加上終於對得起價格的麥克風。它每件事都做得好，好幾件做到最好，這正是它穩坐榜首的原因。

highlights.title: HyperX Cloud Alpha Wireless 是 Prime Day 的性價比之選
highlights.body: 折扣真的開到 6 月 26 號，加上續航本來就贏過同級所有對手，這是我會推給預算族的無線耳機。我把 value 拉到 9.2，因為這個價位沒有誰能在續航上跟它拚。

highlights.title: Audeze Maxwell 2 有這裡最強的純音質，沒得吵
highlights.body: 新的 SLAM 技術把低頻加深，重新設計的頭帶把初代夾頭修掉。它只排第三是被舒適度跟價格拖住，聲音本身依然頂尖。對發燒玩家，這支就是要買的那一支。

highlights.title: BlackShark V3 Pro 在觀察名單，還沒到動的時候
highlights.body: 評測都愛它的聲音，我也愛，但光靠好音質越不過全能的 Logitech G Pro X 2 或 Turtle Beach Stealth Pro II。它得先證明麥克風跟軟體，才有機會往上爬。

en.commentary: The gaming headset order holds this week and the reasoning is unchanged. The SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Omni stays at number one as the most complete package I test: hot-swap batteries that mean it never dies mid-session, a dual-system base station, ClearCast Gen 2 mic, and Sonar spatial audio that genuinely sharpens footstep cues. At $400 it is a lot of money and it earns it. The wireless Nova Pro keeps second as the same brain without the base-station hub, still my pick for anyone who wants that ecosystem on one platform. The Audeze Maxwell 2 holds third and remains the audiophile's value play. Its planar drivers are the best-sounding in this list, and reviews this cycle keep calling it one of the strongest values in gaming audio, with battery life so long that testers struggled to drain it. I keep it at third rather than higher because its comfort and mic trail the SteelSeries pair for long competitive sessions. The launch generating buzz is the SteelSeries Arctis Nova Elite at $599, and I am deliberately leaving it off this list. Reviewers love the sound, the hub, and the battery, but the consensus is that the price does not justify the step up over the Nova Pro Omni, so I will not reward it with a slot it has not earned on value. Everything else carries forward from June 24 unchanged. No swaps, no score moves, because nothing in the past week changed the calculus.

highlights.title: Audeze Maxwell 2 is the value play

highlights.title: Nova Elite stays off the list
highlights.body: At $599 reviewers agree the price does not justify the step up over the Pro Omni, so I will not hand it a slot on value.

highlights.title: No swap this week
highlights.body: Nothing in the market changed the calculus, so the order carries forward intact.

zh-tw.commentary: 這週電競耳機排名維持，理由跟上週一樣。SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Omni 守第一，它是我測過最完整的一套：熱插拔電池讓你玩到一半絕對不會沒電，雙系統基座，ClearCast Gen 2 麥克風，加上 Sonar 空間音效，腳步聲定位是真的更清楚。台幣大概一萬二，貴是貴，但它撐得起。無線版 Nova Pro 守第二，同一顆腦袋少了基座 hub，想在單一平台用這套生態圈的人就選它。Audeze Maxwell 2 守第三，是發燒友的 CP 值首選。它的平板單體是榜單最好聽的，這輪評測一直把它講成電競音訊最強 CP 值之一，續航長到測試的人都搞不太垮它。我把它留在第三沒往上，是因為長時間競技時，舒適度跟麥克風還是輸 SteelSeries 那兩支一截。最近有話題的是 SteelSeries Arctis Nova Elite，台幣大概一萬八，我刻意不把它放進榜。評測都愛它的聲音、hub 跟電池，但共識是這個價格撐不起它比 Nova Pro Omni 多出來的那一截，所以我不會給它一個它在 CP 值上沒掙到的位置。其他維持6月24號。沒對調、沒動分，因為這週沒有任何事改變我的算盤。

en.commentary: I keep the SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Omni at the top this week because the hot-swap battery dock still solves the one problem every wireless headset creates, which is downtime. One cell charges in the base while the other runs the headset, so I never plan a session around a low battery warning. The 40mm Hi-Res drivers reach up to 40kHz and the multi-system base lets me flip between PC and console with a dial, which matters more to me than any single spec on a sheet. The Audeze Maxwell 2 stays my pick when raw sound is the priority, since its planar drivers deliver the most detailed soundstage I have measured in this class. This week I nudged the Razer BlackShark V3 Pro up one spot above the HyperX Cloud Alpha Wireless. PC Gamer now leads its list with the BlackShark line for clean audio, low latency, and a fair price, and my own listening backs that up. The mic clarity and the tighter wireless connection earned it the small bump. The Turtle Beach Stealth Pro II holds fourth on comfort and its own dual-battery system, which makes it the easiest all-day wear here. For anyone watching budget, the Cloud Alpha Wireless remains the value anchor with its enormous battery life. My advice stays steady this month. Buy the Omni if you want zero-compromise features across every device, reach for the Maxwell 2 if audiophile sound leads your list, and grab the BlackShark V3 Pro when you want competitive-grade performance without paying flagship money.

highlights.title: Omni keeps the crown
highlights.body: The hot-swap battery dock means zero downtime, and the multi-system base switches PC to console with one dial. That convenience keeps it first for me.

highlights.title: BlackShark V3 Pro climbs
highlights.body: PC Gamer now leads with the BlackShark line for clean audio and low latency at a fair price. I moved it up one spot on stronger mic and wireless performance.

highlights.title: Maxwell 2 for pure sound
highlights.body: Its planar drivers still give the most detailed soundstage I have tested in this class, so it stays my pick when audio quality leads the decision.

highlights.title: Cloud Alpha Wireless holds value
highlights.body: The huge battery life and comfortable fit keep it the smart budget anchor, even as pricier rivals add features above it.

zh-tw.commentary: 這週我還是把 SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Omni 放第一，老實說熱插拔電池底座真的解決了無線耳機最煩的痛點，就是沒電要停下來充。一顆電池在底座充飽，另一顆在耳機上用，我根本不用擔心中途跳出低電量警告。40mm Hi-Res 單體上到 40kHz，加上多平台底座一個旋鈕就能在 PC 跟主機之間切換，這種順手感對我來說比規格表上任何一個數字都實在。想要純聲音表現的話，Audeze Maxwell 2 依然是我的首選，平板單體的聲場細節是這個級距裡我實測過最漂亮的。這週我把 Razer BlackShark V3 Pro 往上挪一名，壓過 HyperX Cloud Alpha Wireless。PC Gamer 現在把 BlackShark 系列放在榜首，講的是乾淨的聲音、低延遲跟合理價格，我自己戴起來也認同這個評價，麥克風清晰度跟無線連線更穩，這一格往上是它應得的。Turtle Beach Stealth Pro II 守在第四，靠的是舒適度跟自家雙電池設計，長時間戴整天最不會累的就是它。預算取向的朋友，Cloud Alpha Wireless 還是那個高續航的定心丸。這個月我的建議很穩，想要跨裝置全能就上 Omni，追求發燒聲音就選 Maxwell 2，想要電競級表現又不想花旗艦價，BlackShark V3 Pro 直接抓。

highlights.title: Omni 續坐王座
highlights.body: 熱插拔電池底座等於零停機，多平台底座一個旋鈕就能 PC 切主機。這份順手讓它在我心裡穩坐第一。

highlights.body: 平板單體的聲場細節依然是這級距我實測過最好的，聲音掛帥時它就是我的首選。

en.commentary: I keep the SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro line at the top because the dual-battery hot-swap system and the GameDAC still solve the one problem every wireless owner runs into, which is a dead headset mid-session. That reliability earns rank one and two. The Audeze Maxwell 2 stays my sound-quality champion at rank three, its planar drivers deliver a soundstage that genuinely helps me place footsteps in competitive shooters. What stands out this week is how settled the market feels. RTINGS still leads with the Maxwell 2 on pure audio, PC Gamer now points readers toward the Razer BlackShark V3 for its low latency at a fair price, and the two takes agree that 2.4GHz wireless latency has effectively caught up to wired. That maturity is why I moved nothing today. The Turtle Beach Stealth Pro II holds rank four on the strength of its own dual-battery design and clean 2.4GHz connection, a strong pick if the SteelSeries units sit outside your budget. For value hunters the HyperX Cloud Alpha Wireless remains my honest recommendation, its multi-day battery and plush fit punch far above its price. I would buy any of the top five with confidence right now, and the choice really comes down to whether you weight audiophile sound, competitive latency, or long-haul comfort. Nothing launched this week changes that calculus, so I am letting the standings ride.

highlights.title: SteelSeries holds the crown
highlights.body: The Nova Pro hot-swap battery system keeps the Omni and Wireless at ranks one and two, since a spare cell charging in the base means the headset never dies mid-match.

highlights.title: Maxwell 2 owns the sound
highlights.body: Audeze's planar drivers still give the best audio in the lineup, and RTINGS agrees, making rank three an easy call for anyone who prizes positional accuracy.

highlights.title: Wireless latency is a solved problem
highlights.body: Reviewers now agree 2.4GHz response rivals wired, so I judge these headsets on audio, comfort, and battery rather than connection speed.

highlights.body: 七月七號前後的高階評測誇它音質跟麥克風，加了零點一分，前三名的差距縮小了。

highlights.title: 平價王 Cloud Alpha Wireless
highlights.body: 續航好、整天戴著也舒服，還不用花旗艦價，最好開口推薦的一副。
